signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and stable, but the board remains no-trade and the CPI caution window argues against forcing fresh exposure. Neutral is still the right stance.
ETH is mildly constructive but still low-edge, with a neutral board and no compelling directional asymmetry. Not enough to matter before CPI.
SOL is modestly constructive, but positive funding and a no-trade board keep it below threshold. Better to stand down than to force it.
TAO is oversold with negative funding and real liquidity, which makes it interesting, but the board remains mixed and the caution window keeps it below entry threshold.
WLFI has some reversal ingredients with strongly negative funding and crowded positioning, but the board is still mixed rather than cleanly bullish. Watchlist only.

MINA has a legitimate squeeze-continuation profile with strong 24h momentum, deeply negative funding, and a bullish trade flag. Confidence is still capped because liquidity is thin, spread is ugly, and CPI risk is close.
ZEREBRO has momentum, but funding is positive, the market is thin, and the board is still not clean enough to justify a fresh trade under CAUTION.
ARB has acceptable liquidity and some strength, but funding is positive and the board remains neutral. Interesting, not actionable.

FARTCOIN remains the cleaner bearish lean on the board with a large negative 24h move and a bearish mechanical profile. It is volatile, so conviction stays moderate, but short is still better than long here.

TNSR still screens as the strongest continuation long in the set with very strong momentum, deeply negative funding, and a bullish trade flag. CAUTION keeps this as reduced-size only rather than a full-conviction chase.
CHILLGUY is moving, but funding is positive and the market is still too thin and too unstable to trust into a macro event window.
